Only a few variations I made to this was that i used a pork loin cut into small strips, also I used mixture of the canton noodles as stated along with bihon noodles as well love them both so why not use both!!!!! And also put alot more soy sauce adjusted to my liking but I didn't have any green onion or lemon so I just left those out. Made this twice already thanks for such an easy and authentic recipe.

I followed the recipe exactly and didn't like how the tofu turned out. It didn't have much flavor or a nice texture. I used extra-firm tofu with the water squeezed out and it still came out a little crumbly. Maybe it should be cooked on its own in the wok to brown the sides before putting in the sauce, or a marinade could make it more flavorful.  The sauce was very good though, and the vegetables came out great. This recipe would be tasty with shrimp or chicken.  I might try this again with a different method of cooking the tofu or with a light meat.

YUM!  My husband and I do not like vegetables in our rice, so I added only the eggs to the rice.  I only used about 1/4 cup of butter, since I was only mixing the butter with the rice and eggs.  I will definitely add onions next time, i just didn't have any on hand.  Great rice...I served with bourbon chicken, Recipe #45809.

These muffins are moist and light...just delicious.  I used a food processor although I was skeptical about mixing muffin batter this way.  The muffins couldn't have been more tender.  They melted in your mouth.  I did have a problem with the blueberries sinking to the bottom and then sticking when I went to remove the muffins.  The next time I'll dust them with flour to see if this helps.
These were my childhood Christmas cookies! Fantastic flavor and I'm so glad to see them posted!
Great dip! This is perfect for munching on veggies throughout the day. You just made my diet :)
Very good!!  it makes a great season salt thank you!

I made this for dinner last night with 2 "thick cut" pork chops. I browned them in a small Dutch oven, added the other ingredients (I used fresh grated ginger and fresh garlic) then placed the pan into the oven for the baking step. I did however, put the cover on the pan because of the thickness of the chops and possible longer baking time. They  came out very tender and juicy. We liked the sauce and wished there had been more. This was an easy to follow recipe with ingredients readily available on the shelf.

Fresh!  Flavorful!  Fantastic!  This is a deliciously addictive way to get your daily serving of vegetables.  I used Good Seasons Italian Dressing that I made with Filippo Berio Extra Light Tasting Olive Oil (my favorite for dressings) and Marukan Rice Vineagar.  I reduced the recipe for two.  Thank you for posting this recipe.
The size of the pieces makes a difference. 1/4" is small enough that it shouldn't take as long to cook as the cubes that are almost an inch .

I've not prepared this particular recipe, but I've made something similar to it by adding chopped ham and staple seasonings.  It's one of my family's all time favorite, from my 21 yr. old down to my 3 yr. old....It's what one would refer to as good ol' comfort food.

My husband's specialty is omlet's. I had seen this recipe and put it in my Zaar cookbook. He made them this morning, all I can say is OMG!!! Why hasnt someone reviewed these before this?? This is a wonderful dish! :):)
Nice and simple.  Very tasty.
This was a good tasting cookie. No problems with saltiness or dryness of the cookie. My DS enjoyed them!

This was so easy! First time baking with rhubarb i was a bit scared but everything was fine :) But Seriously, guys, why hasnt anyone rated this yet? anyways my family thought it was very yummy. altho, as usualy i did make some adjustments: instead the oil i substituted more applesauce rounding it off to 1/2 cup applesauce, and i didnt add any walnuts coz we had none. After baking it i thought it might have needed a bit more time, but it just ends up being really moist and almost pudding-like. AND the heavenly cake smelled like those mini dounuts from the carnival coming out of the oven hehe! but everyone thought this was excellent, thanks Karen!

The Flat Leaf Parsley did not flavor the dish.  I think it is just my personal ho-hum opinion of the herb.  I will try again but with Basil or my favorite Oregano.  The Garlic also did not do much for me as whole cloves and no one would eat the clove.  Next time I will chop the garlic.  Don't know why so much Olive Oil, but I will not use so much next time, if any.  Good starter recipe and very easy to customize.
So Tasty!  Will definitely make it again soon!

very good tasting, but mine was runny, like soup. maybe I didn't let it cook down long enough at each point. and I used diced tomatoes rather than crushed. hubby still wants leftovers for lunch tomorrow though, so it's a keeper!
So delicious, packed full of wonderful flavor combinations. Can't wait to make this for my DD when she comes to visit this summer!
Easy to make and very yummy!  I made two rounds, as Amazonheart suggested.  One round was plain, the other had a sugar topping.  I like both!  I can't wait to experiment with other flavorings.
DH loved this. I will be making this dish again.

This was very nice, and I'll definitely make it again. I found it to be too bitter when I first tasted it, but adding sugar and cooking the tomatoes down more helped. I reduced the oil to 3 tbsp. and will probably cut back to 2 tbsp. next time. Thanks!
This didn't taste any different than any other quick bread. I may try again, with more coconut.

These cookies were a wonderful addition to my cookie plates for all the neighbors and family this year.  I have gotten so many compliments on them!  This cookie is a new favorite of mine and I will be making them time and again in the future.  They do take time to make but worth every minute and they do make a ton of luscious cookies for any time of year.  Thank you Linda for this special cookie recipe.  Arlene

Loved it !!! The recipe is wonderful as is, but next time, I will use only 1/2 tsp. of the pepper seeds. I did use anchovy fillets instead of paste and only about 1 Tbsp. of oil, 1/4 c is a lot. This was served with fresh linguini, a crisp salad and toasted Ciabatta bread with garlic butter. Yummy. The sauce was very easy to put together just before dinner but there is no reason it could not be made ahead of time if it is to be doubled or more for a company dinner.
This was really great. Easy and fast. I did cut back on the milk. Outside of that, I followed the recipe. I served it with mashed red skinned potatoes, and peas, hmm.
This dish was excellent and went great served along side Brats and Onions! Thanks for a great recipe!
I used brown sugar in place of granulated and used a gingersnap cookie crust

When I can get all four kids to agree that a meal is delicious, I have a winner, and that is just what happened when I made this soup for supper last night. Thank you, ravensmith for sharing this yummy recipe. Oh, I left out the Tabasco sauce as I didn't have any, and it was still delicious.
Oh my gosh! This is delicious!! I could eat it by the spoonful. Thanks so much for sharing this great recipe!!

These are amazing! I make them all the time now.
Light yet satisfying.

Yum! I could eat a whole meal of these! I ended up having to tap a coconut and making my own coconut milk, which was delicious (!) and definitely worth the work for this recipe. My kids liked this too. Thanks!

Looking over the ingredients list, I decided to use Chinese shao xing wine instead of sherry for a more authentic taste. Chicken was so tender and tasty. Preparation was so easy. Thank you for posting.

This was easy and delicious - I used salmon fillets and oven baked it for 25 minutes.  I'd never thought of using pesto and salmon together before - thanks for the inspiration, Fairy! :-) Made for Recipe Swap July 2008 in the Australian/NZ forum.
I make these smoothies all the time. They are nutritious and delicious. My favorites are with strawberries or an orange which must be pureed to grind up the pulp.
I LOVE this recipe! I always get so many great comments when I make this.  One tip is to not make it too far in advance cause the apples will start to brown & not look as pretty.  Very easy to make.
I absolutely love this recipe! I leave out the cilantro, and it is still very flavorful and filling. I can even get my 4 year old to like it! A big hit!

Loved your recipe!  I did make a couple of changes.  I used buttermilk, since I had some to use up, and used cayenne instead of paprika...otherwise the same and came out good...also have to make sure dogs are very dry and Alton Browns hint of rolling in cornstarch first before dipping in batter might help to make the batter stick.  I am Armenian and love the Greek and Middle Eastern stuff too!
Everyone thought I bought this from the store!  VERY TASTY!
These were the best Pancakes I've ever made.  Light and fluffy (I think due to beating the eggs with an electric mixer) and not too sweet.
Amazing tatse, nice and thick.
This recipe was wounderful,muffins came out great using splenda.might add crushed pineapple next time to make them more moist. I made texas muffins. thanks for recipe

When combining, I left the hot sauce out because my husband can't really eat spicy food.  I love spicy so I just got myself a bowl of salad, a scoop of your tuna salad, and dashed away with the hot sauce. Before, I used to add Tapatio when eating tuna salad, but Tabasco wasn't a bad substitute.  The hot sauce you use makes a difference~

A wonderful recipe! The fish came out incredibly flaky and had a terrific balance of flavors -- even though I had no parsley and had to substitute lemon juice for the lime juice (all I had on hand). It didn't "crisp" very much, but that's probably because I don't often have real butter in the house, prefering a more heart-healthy alternative such as Smart Balance spread. I think that real butter would have improved the texture. The flavor, however, was oustanding nevertheless! Will definitely make again. Thanks for posting it!
In Italy I think this is called a "Caprice" Salad. It's one of our favs and yes - fresh Mozzarella and Fresh Basil is a must. Best in the summer when all those vine ripened tomatoes are in season.

Not sure what happened, but second time making this cake just turned out terrible! Center was all gooey and did not bake properly at all, depsite 45 minutes of cooking.
This was a disaster and had to be thrown out!!

Just made my second batch...a big hit with my brownie loving children. I was sceptical at first as the mixture was so dry I had to litrally spread the mixture into the pan. But do not fear they turned out so well. I liked the fact they use cocoa powder; I always have that to hand

When I put this out on St. Patty's night, my family basically did a nosedive into their plates! Those potatoes were the single greatest hit of the evening. I know I shall have to make just those alone many more times to come! By-the-way, the whole meal was to die for as well!  (We gave the carrot pieces to our dogs, who don't claim a particular religion. We felt that was safe!) Thanks so much for sharing. They may not eat Corned Beef and Cabbage in Ireland, but I do know we love it here in Jersey!

This was pretty good. Next time I will not wait for the cake to cool down before adding the milk/fudge. They both just sat on top of the cake and didn't really seep in. Also, made it hard to get the whip cream to stay put. Good flavor though. I think it will be even better next time. Thanks!

We used this as a topping for pasta. We cooked some sliced Jalapeno Chicken Sausage along with the peppers and this gave it some more flavor. I think it would have been bland had we not done that. We'll probably make this again. It's very easy. Thanks for posting.
I enjoyed this recipe.  I used frozen spinach instead of fresh.  The serving size says 4 but it served our 6.

This turned out very nice!!  Such a lovely texture and the flavor was great as well.  I served with crackers & carrot sticks.  We tried some unbaked and think that was just as good!!  I used two little ramekins to bake them in and they turned out great.  I didn't keep them on the high temp the whole time though because they did start to brown too much.  I would just recommend peaking in about half way to make sure your not overcooking.  Made for Veg*n Swap 31!!  Thanks so much WICC!!

Your recipe is very close to one I do from a Mennonite cookbook.  I thought my recipe was good but my son thinks yours might be just a bit better...can you believe that? 
I did include the chocolate chips for the kids. Thx.
